lenses.guidance: The 'place the LORD will choose' refrain (vv 5, 11, 14, 18, 21, 26) becomes the canonical thread that runs to Jerusalem's temple, then dissolves at Jn 4:21 — neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem, but in spirit and truth.

vv 5-14's one-sanctuary principle prefigures the singular access-point. Jn 2:19-21 names Christ as the temple, the type fulfilled — the place where God meets his people is no longer a location but a person.

vv 1-4's destroy-the-high-places command makes covenant purity the precondition of inheritance. Mixed worship halts the redemption arc; the conquest of land is also the conquest of cult, with the gods of Canaan first.
